memory:memory/2026-04-15.md:39:46.snippet: - JT7 platform architecture must treat all bots/threads as conversation surfaces on one unified platform: one engine, one gateway, one shared brain, no forked systems, and no duplicated logic. - Platform agent is the control plane and owns routing, system rules, orchestration, and decisions about where work should go. - Domain agents are worker agents; each thread defaults to its assigned domain agent and should not behave like a general assistant. - Required platform behavior: determine whether a request belongs to the current thread domain, execute in-thread when it fits, and create an explicit structured handoff when it does not. - Handoffs must be explicit, explain why work is moving, pr
